mappings.rationaleAI: The Arab conquests' spread of Islam into North Africa from the 7th century provides essential context for A.2 (spread of Muslim domination from c. 639 into the Nilotic Sudan).

The Prester John legend's association with Ethiopia and its influence on European exploration directly correspond to B.3 on contacts with the Portuguese (1520-c. 1632) and European perceptions of the Ethiopian Christian kingdom.

Coffee's origins in Ethiopia and Yemen provide context for the region's economic significance and cultural connections to the broader Islamic world, relevant to the Nilotic Sudan and Ethiopian trade networks.
